LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARIN COUNTRY DAY SCHOOL, STEVEN HARRISON'S MOTION TO OPPOSE CROSS DEFENDANTS APPLICATION THAT SETTLEMENT WAS MADE IN GOOD FAITH. A R G U E D. COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANTS TSENJJIAV JIGJIDSUREN AND SERGELEN GANUTUMUR'S APPLICATION FOR A GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT DETERMINATION OF THEIR SETTLEMENT WITH PLAINTIFF JEFFREY BRUNECKER PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 877.6 IS GRANTED AND DEFENDANTS MARIN COUNTRY DAY SCHOOL AND STEVEN HARRISON'S MOTION IN OPPOSITION IS DENIED. SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR THE COURT'S RULING AT LENGTH. PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS APPROVED AS TO FORM AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Oppose Cross Defendants Application That Settlement Was Made In Good Faith
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alexander I. MacDougall, Esq., Law Offices of Catherine Walsh, Defendant/Cross Defendant Jigjidsuren, Tsendjjav,
Shamika K. Bains, Esq., Norton & Melnik, Esa., 925-718-1040, appearing for Defendant(s) Marin County Day School.

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MARIN COUNTRY DAY SCHOOL, STEVEN HARRISON'S Motion To Oppose Cross Defendants Application That Settlement Was Made In Good Faith.

Cross-defendants Tsenjjiav Jigjidsuren and Sergelen Ganutumur's application for a good faith settlement determination of their settlement with plaintiff Jeffrey Brunecker pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 877.6 is granted and Defendants Marin Country Day School and Steven Harrison's motion in opposition is denied. Cross-defendants have standing to seek a good faith determination of their settlement with plaintiff, even though they did not make an appearance as defendants before they were dismissed by plaintiff. (See Great Western Bank v. Converse Consultants, Inc. (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 609, 613 ["A cross-defendant who has not been named as a defendant in the main action may enter into a good faith settlement with the plaintiff in the main action and obtain a dismissal of the cross-complaint pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6."]; County of Los Angeles v. Guerrero (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1149, 1152 ["The fact the settlement was entered into well before the filing of suit does not preclude a finding of good faith."].) There is no bar to seeking a determination after the finalization of the settlement. (See Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 822, 829 ["Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6 provides no time limit after settlement within which a motion for determination of good faith of the settlement must be brought."].) Defendants have not met their burden to show that the settlement was not entered into in good faith. (Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(d).) The court finds that the amount of the settlement based on the information available to the settling parties at time of settlement is not grossly disproportionate to a reasonable estimate of settling cross-defendants' liability and that the settlement was the result of arm's-length bargaining in good faith. (Helser Decl. 5, 6; see Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assocs. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.)

Counsel for Cross-defendant is to prepare and submit a proposed order that is approved as to form and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)
